name: Geography
description: Discover capitals, continents, rivers, mountains, and world landmarks.

questions.text: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: Canberra is the capital of Australia, chosen as a compromise between rivals Sydney and Melbourne.

questions.text: Which is the largest continent by area?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent, covering about 44.6 million km² and home to over 4.7 billion people.

questions.text: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River, flowing through northeastern Africa, is widely considered the world's longest river at about 6,650 km.

questions.text: Which country has the largest population in the world?
questions.explanation: India surpassed China in 2023 to become the world's most populous country, with over 1.4 billion people.

questions.text: What is the capital of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital and largest city of Japan, and one of the world's most populous metropolitan areas.

questions.text: Which ocean is the largest?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest ocean, covering more than 165 million km².

questions.text: Mount Everest is located in which mountain range?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, the world's highest peak at 8,849 m, is located in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border.

questions.text: What is the capital of Brazil?
questions.explanation: Brasília has been the capital of Brazil since 1960, replacing Rio de Janeiro as part of a planned development initiative.

questions.text: Which country contains the Amazon Rainforest's largest portion?
questions.explanation: Brazil contains about 60% of the Amazon Rainforest, the world's largest tropical rainforest.

questions.text: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City, covering just 0.44 km², is the smallest sovereign state in the world by both area and population.

questions.text: Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert in North Africa is the world's largest hot desert, covering about 9.2 million km².

questions.text: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital of Canada, located in the province of Ontario near the Quebec border.

questions.text: On which continent is Egypt located?
questions.explanation: Egypt is located in northeastern Africa, though the Sinai Peninsula extends into Asia.

questions.text: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France, known for landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and the Louvre.

questions.text: Which country has the most natural lakes in the world?
questions.explanation: Canada has more lakes than any other country, containing about 60% of the world's fresh lakes.

questions.text: What is the tallest mountain in Africa?
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania is the highest peak in Africa at 5,895 meters above sea level.

questions.text: Which city is the capital of Argentina?
questions.explanation: Buenos Aires is the capital and largest city of Argentina, located on the shores of the Río de la Plata.

questions.text: What is the capital of South Africa? (It has three capitals; name the administrative one.)
questions.explanation: Pretoria is South Africa's administrative capital; Cape Town is the legislative capital and Bloemfontein is the judicial capital.

questions.text: Through how many countries does the Danube River flow?
questions.explanation: The Danube flows through 10 countries: Germany, Austria, Slovakia, Hungary, Croatia, Serbia, Romania, Bulgaria, Moldova, and Ukraine.

questions.text: Which country owns the Galápagos Islands?
questions.explanation: The Galápagos Islands are an archipelago belonging to Ecuador, famous for inspiring Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.

questions.text: What is the name of the world's largest coral reef system?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef off the coast of Queensland, Australia, is the world's largest coral reef system, stretching over 2,300 km.

questions.text: Which strait separates Europe from Africa at the Mediterranean's western end?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Spain (Europe) from Morocco (Africa) and connects the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.text: What is the capital of Indonesia?
questions.explanation: Indonesia officially moved its capital from Jakarta to Nusantara (on Borneo island) in 2024.

questions.text: Which African country is entirely surrounded by South Africa?
questions.explanation: Lesotho is a landlocked country completely surrounded by South Africa, making it one of only three such enclaved nations in the world.

questions.text: What is the largest lake in Africa?
questions.explanation: Lake Victoria, shared by Uganda, Kenya, and Tanzania, is Africa's largest lake by surface area.

questions.text: Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
questions.explanation: Canada has the world's longest coastline at approximately 202,080 km, including its mainland and islands.

questions.text: What is the capital of Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Islamabad has been the capital of Pakistan since 1967, replacing Karachi as a planned city.

questions.text: The Atacama Desert is located primarily in which country?
questions.explanation: The Atacama Desert is located mostly in northern Chile and is one of the driest places on Earth.

questions.text: Which river is the longest in South America?
questions.explanation: The Amazon River is the longest in South America (~6,400 km) and the largest by water discharge in the world.

questions.text: What is the name of the deepest lake in the world?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal in Siberia, Russia, is the world's deepest lake at 1,642 meters and contains about 20% of Earth's fresh surface water.

questions.text: Which country is both the origin and the end point of the Nile River?
options: It originates in Uganda and empties in Egypt
It originates in Sudan and empties in Egypt
It originates in Ethiopia and empties in Egypt
It originates in Tanzania and empties in Sudan
questions.explanation: The White Nile originates near Lake Victoria in Uganda and Tanzania; it flows northward through Sudan and Egypt before emptying into the Mediterranean.

questions.text: How many countries share a land border with Germany?
questions.explanation: Germany borders 9 countries: Denmark, Poland, Czech Republic, Austria, Switzerland, France, Luxembourg, Belgium, and the Netherlands.

questions.text: What is the capital of Kenya?
questions.explanation: Nairobi is the capital and largest city of Kenya and serves as a major hub for East Africa.

questions.text: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains in Russia are traditionally considered one of the main geographical boundaries between Europe and Asia.

questions.text: In which country is the Dead Sea located?
questions.explanation: The Dead Sea borders Israel, Jordan, and the West Bank (Palestinian territories), and is the lowest point on Earth's surface.

questions.text: What is the capital of Kazakhstan, the world's largest landlocked country?
questions.explanation: The capital of Kazakhstan is Astana (formerly Nur-Sultan), which replaced Almaty as the capital in 1997.

questions.text: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to its overseas territories spread around the globe, more than any other country.

questions.text: What is the name of the tectonic plate that India sits on?
questions.explanation: India rests on the Indo-Australian Plate, whose collision with the Eurasian Plate formed the Himalayas.

questions.text: What is the name of the world's longest mountain range, mostly underwater?
questions.explanation: The Mid-Ocean Ridge system is the world's longest mountain range at ~65,000 km, mostly submerged beneath the ocean.

questions.text: Which African country has the most pyramids?
questions.explanation: Sudan (ancient Nubia) has more than 200 pyramids—more than Egypt—built by the rulers of the ancient Kushite kingdoms.

questions.text: What is the capital of Bhutan?
questions.explanation: Thimphu is the capital and largest city of Bhutan, situated in the western central part of the country.

questions.text: The Strait of Malacca connects which two bodies of water?
options: South China Sea and the Bay of Bengal
Andaman Sea and the Java Sea
Indian Ocean and the Pacific Ocean
South China Sea and the Indian Ocean
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca connects the South China Sea to the Indian Ocean and is one of the world's busiest shipping lanes.

questions.text: Which country contains the geographic center of Europe (commonly cited near Sumen, Belarus)?
questions.explanation: The geographic center of Europe is often cited as being in Belarus, near the town of Polotsk or Suman, depending on the calculation method.

questions.text: What is the name of the current that moderates the climate of Northwest Europe?
questions.explanation: The Gulf Stream is a warm Atlantic Ocean current that flows from the Gulf of Mexico to Europe, keeping northwest European climates milder.

questions.text: What is the capital of Myanmar (Burma)?
questions.explanation: Naypyidaw became Myanmar's capital in 2006, replacing Yangon as part of the military government's plan to build a new administrative center.

questions.text: The Mariana Trench, the deepest point on Earth, is located in which ocean?
questions.explanation: The Mariana Trench is located in the western Pacific Ocean near the Mariana Islands and reaches a depth of about 11,034 meters.

questions.text: Which country has the highest average elevation?
questions.explanation: The Tibet Autonomous Region of China has the highest average elevation of any region at over 4,500 meters above sea level.

questions.text: What is the name of the vast, flat, elevated plateau in South America known as the 'roof of the Americas'?
questions.explanation: The Altiplano is a high plateau in the Andes, spanning Bolivia and Peru, averaging about 3,750 m above sea level.

questions.text: Which country contains the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
questions.explanation: Italy leads the world with 58 UNESCO World Heritage Sites as of 2024, followed closely by China.

questions.text: What is the Coriolis effect in geography?
options: The warming of ocean currents near the equator
The deflection of winds and currents due to Earth's rotation
The rising of warm air over land masses
The melting of polar ice due to heat transfer
questions.explanation: The Coriolis effect is the deflection of moving air and water caused by Earth's rotation — to the right in the Northern Hemisphere and left in the Southern.
